requirement: When EWA is 0, the write completion response for the transaction must come from the endpoint.

requirement: It is permitted, but not required, for an implementation not to use the EWA attribute.

title: A Read transaction must not read more data than requested.

requirement: A read must get its data from the endpoint.

title: Accesses to Device memory must use the following types, exclusive variants are permitted:
requirement: Accesses to Device memory must use the following types, exclusive variants are permitted:

title: Read accesses to a Device memory location must use ReadNoSnp .
requirement: Read accesses to a Device memory location must use ReadNoSnp .

requirement: Write accesses to a Device memory location must use WriteNoSnpPtl , WriteNoSnpFull , WriteNoSnpZero ,

title: Atomic* transactions are permitted to Device memory locations.
requirement: Atomic* transactions are permitted to Device memory locations.

title: The Cacheable attribute indicates if a transaction must perform a cache lookup:
requirement: The Cacheable attribute indicates if a transaction must perform a cache lookup:

title: When Cacheable is 1, the transaction must perform a cache lookup.
condition: When Cacheable is 1, the transaction must perform a cache lookup.
requirement: When Cacheable is 1, the transaction must perform a cache lookup.

title: When Cacheable is 0, the transaction must access the final destination.
condition: When Cacheable is 0, the transaction must access the final destination.
requirement: When Cacheable is 0, the transaction must access the final destination.

title: However, the cache is permitted to not allocate the transaction.
requirement: However, the cache is permitted to not allocate the transaction.

title: However, the cache is permitted to allocate the transaction.
requirement: However, the cache is permitted to allocate the transaction.
